title: permission_handler
objectives: Handle 'permanently denied' with a settings redirect

sections.markdown: `permission_handler` unifies runtime permissions across iOS and Android: request them, check status, and route the user to app settings when they've permanently denied.
Request in-context: ask when the user taps the feature that needs it, not on app launch. Explain WHY before showing the OS prompt — better acceptance rates and less rejection risk from Apple review.

Missing Info.plist keys (iOS) or AndroidManifest permissions — request calls silently fail. Also: not handling `permanentlyDenied` — the OS won't show a prompt again; you must guide the user to settings.
Group related permissions and request only what you use. On Android 13+ POST_NOTIFICATIONS is a runtime permission; on iOS `criticalAlert` needs an entitlement.

flashcards.back: User denied and (Android: 'Don't ask again' / iOS: after prior denial) — the OS won't show the prompt; open settings.
Add the usage description keys to Info.plist (NSCameraUsageDescription, etc.).
Request in context of a user action, with a pre-prompt explaining the reason.

quiz.explain: OS won't prompt again; user must change it in Settings.
iOS silently fails without the usage description.

options: When user taps the feature (with pre-prompt)
quiz.explain: In-context requests have higher acceptance rates.

challenges.text: Request camera permission before opening camera.
Show a pre-prompt explaining why; handle denial gracefully.
Handle permanentlyDenied with a Settings redirect + re-check on resume.
Add POST_NOTIFICATIONS (Android 13+) and iOS notification permission with fallback UX.

interviewQuestions: iOS Info.plist usage descriptions — why required?
Best UX pattern for permission requests?
